title: Interpreting quartiles
description: Practice understanding the meaning of quartiles of data sets.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following table shows the five-number summary for the number of band members by high school in Orange County.

$\text{Min}$ |$Q_1$ |$\text{Median}$ |$Q_3$ |$\text{Max}$
:-: | :-: | :-:| :-:| :-:
$80$|$90$|$105$|$115$|$130$

**What percent of high schools in Orange County have more than $130$ band members?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Each **quartile** helps us split the data points into fourths.

Value | Approximate fraction of data above this value | Approximate percentage of data above this value
-|-:|-:
Minimum | All | $100\%$
$Q_1$ | $\dfrac{3}{4}$ | $75\%$
Median |  $\dfrac{2}{4}$, which is $\dfrac{1}{2}$ | $50\%$
$Q_3$ | $\dfrac{1}{4}$ | $25\%$
Maximum | None | $0\%$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\maroonD{130}$ band members is the maximum. So $0\%$ of high schools have *more* than $\maroonD{130}$ members.

|$\text{Min}$ |$Q_1$ |$\text{Median}$ |$Q_3$ |$\text{Max}$
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-:| :-:| :-:
**Five-number summary**|$80$| $90 $| $105$ |  $115$| $\maroonD{\boxed{130}}$
**Approximate percentage of data *above* this value**|$100\%$|$75\%$|$50\%$|$25\%$|$\blueE{\boxed{0\%}}$
$0\%$ of high schools in Orange County have more than $130$ band members.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following table shows the five-number summary for the number of surfers at each beach in Miami.

$\text{Min}$ |$Q_1$ |$\text{Median}$ |$Q_3$ |$\text{Max}$
:-: | :-: | :-:| :-:| :-:
$2$|$8$|$13$|$15$|$19$

**What percent of beaches in Miami have $2$ or more surfers?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Each **quartile** helps us split the data points into fourths.

Value | Approximate fraction of data above this value | Approximate percentage of data above this value
-|-:|-:
Minimum | All | $100\%$
$Q_1$ | $\dfrac{3}{4}$ | $75\%$
Median |  $\dfrac{2}{4}$, which is $\dfrac{1}{2}$ | $50\%$
$Q_3$ | $\dfrac{1}{4}$ | $25\%$
Maximum | None | $0\%$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\maroonD{2}$ surfers is the minimum. So $100\%$ of the beaches have $\maroonD{2}$ or *more* surfers.

|$\text{Min}$ |$Q_1$ |$\text{Median}$ |$Q_3$ |$\text{Max}$
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-:| :-:| :-:
**Five-number summary**|$\maroonD{\boxed{2}}$| $8 $| $13$ |  $15$| $19$
**Approximate percentage of data *above* this value**|$\blueE{\boxed{100\%}}$|$75\%$|$50\%$|$25\%$|$0\%$
$100\%$ of beaches in Miami have $2$ or more surfers.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following table shows the five-number summary for the number of gym members at each gym in Shanesville.

$\text{Min}$ |$Q_1$ |$\text{Median}$ |$Q_3$ |$\text{Max}$
:-: | :-: | :-:| :-:| :-:
$50$|$56$|$64$|$70$|$76$

**About what percent of gyms in Shanesville have fewer than $56$ gym members?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Each **quartile** helps us split the data points into fourths.

Value | Approximate fraction of data below this value | Approximate percentage of data below this value
-|-:|-:
Minimum | None | $0\%$
$Q_1$ | $\dfrac{1}{4}$ | $25\%$
Median |  $\dfrac{2}{4}$, which is $\dfrac{1}{2}$ | $50\%$
$Q_3$ | $\dfrac{3}{4}$ | $75\%$
Maximum | All | $100\%$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\maroonD{56}$ gym members is the first quartile. So about $25\%$ of the gyms have *fewer* than $\maroonD{56}$ members.

|$\text{Min}$ |$Q_1$ |$\text{Median}$ |$Q_3$ |$\text{Max}$
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-:| :-:| :-:
**Five-number summary**|$50$| $\maroonD{\boxed{56}} $| $64$ |  $70 $| $76$
**Approximate percentage of data *below* this value**|$0\%$|$\blueE{\boxed{25\%}}$|$50\%$|$75\%$|$100\%$
About $25\%$ of gyms in Shanesville have fewer than $56$ gym members.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following table shows the five-number summary for the number of aces  Olga served during each tennis match.

$\text{Min}$ |$Q_1$ |$\text{Median}$ |$Q_3$ |$\text{Max}$
:-: | :-: | :-:| :-:| :-:
$0$|$3$|$6$|$10$|$14$

**Olga served more than $6$ aces in about what percent of tennis matches?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Each **quartile** helps us split the data points into fourths.

Value | Approximate fraction of data above this value | Approximate percentage of data above this value
-|-:|-:
Minimum | All | $100\%$
$Q_1$ | $\dfrac{3}{4}$ | $75\%$
Median |  $\dfrac{2}{4}$, which is $\dfrac{1}{2}$ | $50\%$
$Q_3$ | $\dfrac{1}{4}$ | $25\%$
Maximum | None | $0\%$

[[☃ explanation 2]]

$\maroonD{6}$ aces is the median. So about $50\%$ of the matches have *more* than $\maroonD{6}$ aces.

|$\text{Min}$ |$Q_1$ |$\text{Median}$ |$Q_3$ |$\text{Max}$
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-:| :-:| :-:
**Five-number summary**|$0$| $3$| $\maroonD{\boxed{6}}$ |  $10$| $14$
**Approximate percentage of data *above* this value**|$100\%$|$75\%$|$\blueE{\boxed{50\%}}$|$25\%$|$0\%$
Olga served more than $6$ aces in about $50\%$ of tennis matches.
